parameters.content:
## Wazuh AI Security Analyzer

Receives Wazuh webhook alerts (level >= 9 by default), sends the full payload to an LLM for analysis, then delivers an enriched Slack and/or Telegram message with AI risk assessment + investigation commands.

**Quick start:**
1. Set up credentials and the webhook (see "Prerequisites & Resources" sticky)
2. Edit the **Config** node: thresholds, timezone, model, Telegram IDs
3. Customize the infrastructure context block in **Extract Alert** for your environment, this is what makes the analysis actually useful
4. Set the Slack webhook URL in **Slack AI Alert** and/or the Telegram token+chat ID in Config for **Telegram AI Alert**
5. Activate the workflow and point your Wazuh ossec.conf integration at `/webhook/wazuh-alert`

**Default model:** claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 (~$0.001/alert). Switch to claude-sonnet-4-5, GPT-4o, or a local Ollama model for deeper analysis. See "Extract & Analyze" sticky.

**Built by Agenius AI Labs** | github.com/Agenius-AI-Labs

## Prerequisites & Resources

Everything a first-time user needs. Each link goes to the exact setup doc.

**1. n8n** (orchestrator, self-hosted or cloud)
https://docs.n8n.io/hosting/installation/
Cloud signup: https://n8n.io/cloud/

**2. Wazuh SIEM** (alert source)
Install: https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/installation-guide/index.html
Custom integration webhook: https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/user-manual/manager/manual-integration.html

**3. LLM API key, pick one:**
- Anthropic Claude (recommended): https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/getting-started
- OpenAI: https://platform.openai.com/docs/quickstart
- Ollama (local, free): https://ollama.com/download
See "Extract & Analyze" sticky for swap instructions.

**4. Notification channel, pick one or both:**
- Slack incoming webhook: https://api.slack.com/messaging/webhooks
  Recommend creating a dedicated #security-alerts channel first.
- Telegram bot: https://core.telegram.org/bots/tutorial
  Create the bot via @BotFather, get your chat ID via @userinfobot.

No paid services required to run this. Anthropic/OpenAI are pay-per-use (alerts cost a fraction of a cent). Ollama runs fully local.

## Wazuh Incoming Alert Webhook

This webhook receives Wazuh alerts in real time. Configure your Wazuh manager to POST every alert (or a filtered subset) here.

## Configuration & Qualification

Edit the **Config** node to tune behavior. All fields are passed downstream to every node.

## Extract & Analyze



















The **Extract Alert** node builds the LLM prompt. The infrastructure context block (clearly marked with `===` comment banners) contains an EXAMPLE lab. Replace it with your own environment. The more specific (hostnames, services, normal patterns), the better the analysis.

The **Claude Analysis** node is preconfigured for Anthropic. Swap as needed:

## Format & Notify
























**Format AI Message** parses the LLM JSON response and builds both a Slack and Telegram message string. Both notification nodes run in parallel from the same output.

## Extract & Analyze

The **Extract Alert** node builds the LLM prompt. The infrastructure context block (clearly marked with `===` comment banners) contains an EXAMPLE lab. Replace it with your own environment. The more specific (hostnames, services, normal patterns), the better the analysis.

The **Claude Analysis** node is preconfigured for Anthropic. Swap as needed:

**Anthropic Claude (default)**
- URL: `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages`
- Auth: Header Auth credential named `Anthropic API`, header `x-api-key`, value = your key
- Extra header: `anthropic-version: 2023-06-01`
- Models: `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` (cheap), `claude-sonnet-4-5` (deeper)

**OpenAI swap**
- URL: `https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions`
- Auth: Header Auth credential, header `Authorization`, value `Bearer YOUR_OPENAI_KEY`
- Replace `claudeBodyObj` in Extract Alert with:
```
{
  model: 'gpt-4o-mini',
  max_tokens: 700,
  messages: [
    { role: 'system', content: systemPrompt },
    { role: 'user', content: userPrompt }
  ]
}
```
- The Format AI Message node already handles OpenAI's response shape, no further edits needed.

**Ollama swap (local, free)**
- URL: `http://<ollama-host>:11434/api/chat`
- No auth needed
- Replace `claudeBodyObj` in Extract Alert with:
```
{
  model: 'llama3.1:8b',
  stream: false,
  format: 'json',
  messages: [
    { role: 'system', content: systemPrompt },
    { role: 'user', content: userPrompt }
  ]
}
```
- Format AI Message already handles Ollama's response shape.
- Recommended models: `llama3.1:8b`, `qwen2.5:7b`, `mistral:7b`

## Configuration & Qualification

Edit the **Config** node to tune behavior. All fields are passed downstream to every node.

**`minAlertLevel`** (default: 9): Wazuh alerts below this level are silently dropped at the IF node, cheap cost control. Increase to 10-11 in noisy environments or configure another alert notification and triage fork.

**`criticalLevel`** (default: 12): Alerts at or above this get the red 🔴 emoji + CRITICAL label in the message.

**`timezone`** (default: America/New_York): IANA timezone string for the human-readable timestamp.

**`claudeModel`** (default: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001): LLM model to use. See "Extract & Analyze" for alternatives.

**`telegramBotToken`** + **`telegramChatId`**: fill these in if using Telegram. Leave defaults and delete the Telegram branch if Slack-only.

**`IF Level Qualifies`** node: already wired to drop anything below `minAlertLevel`. The "Stop - Below Threshold" branch is a deliberate no-op so you can swap in metrics / logging later if you want to track suppressed alerts.

**To test:** click "Listen for test event" on the Webhook node, then either trigger a real Wazuh alert or POST a sample JSON payload with curl.

## Wazuh Incoming Alert Webhook

This webhook receives Wazuh alerts in real time. Configure your Wazuh manager to POST every alert (or a filtered subset) here.

**1. Get the webhook URL** from the node after saving the workflow:
- Production: `https://<your-n8n-host>/webhook/wazuh-alert`
- Test: `https://<your-n8n-host>/webhook-test/wazuh-alert`

**2. Add the integration to Wazuh** by editing `/var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf` and adding inside `<ossec_config>`:

```xml
<integration>
  <name>custom-n8n</name>
  <hook_url>https://your-n8n-host/webhook/wazuh-alert</hook_url>
  <level>9</level>
  <alert_format>json</alert_format>
</integration>
```

**3. Create the integration script** at `/var/ossec/integrations/custom-n8n` (chmod 750, owner root:wazuh). Wazuh ships sample scripts you can adapt, see:
https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/user-manual/manager/integration-with-external-apis.html

**4. Restart Wazuh:** `systemctl restart wazuh-manager`

**Tip:** start with `<level>9</level>` in ossec.conf, then tune `minAlertLevel` in the Config node to fine-tune what actually reaches the LLM.

## Format & Notify

**Format AI Message** parses the LLM JSON response and builds both a Slack and Telegram message string. Both notification nodes run in parallel from the same output.

**Slack setup**
1. Create an incoming webhook: https://api.slack.com/messaging/webhooks
2. Paste the webhook URL into the **Slack AI Alert** node's `URL` field
3. Recommend a dedicated #security-alerts channel

**Telegram setup**
1. Message @BotFather on Telegram, run `/newbot`, save the bot token
2. Message @userinfobot to get your chat ID (or a group chat ID)
3. Add your bot to the target chat or group
4. Set `telegramBotToken` and `telegramChatId` in the **Config** node
5. Bot API docs: https://core.telegram.org/bots/api#sendmessage

**Using only one channel?**
Delete the branch you don't want. The Format node has no dependency on either notification node, removing one will not break the workflow.

**Want SMS, PagerDuty, Discord, email?**
Add another HTTP Request node downstream of Format AI Message. Discord uses Slack-compatible markdown and can reuse `$json.slackMessage` directly. PagerDuty needs the Events API v2 envelope, wrap accordingly.
